data.title: Tenon Medical Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Tenon Medical Inc reported a quarterly adjusted loss of $2.54​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $-5.44. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for a loss of $2.54 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $-3.77 to $-1.31 per share.

  • Revenue fell 4.7% to $770.00 thousand from a year ago; analysts expected $1.45 million.

  • Tenon Medical Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of $2.54​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $3.09 million.

  • Tenon Medical Inc shares had fallen by 37.2% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the medical equipment, supplies & distribution peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Tenon Medical Inc is $10.00

data.title: Nike Also Navigating Several External Factors That Create Uncertainty, Affect Consumer Confidence, CFO Says
Activist David Hoffmann Proposes Purchase of Lee Enterprises — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

The Hoffmann Family of Cos. is looking to buy Lee Enterprises after increasing its stake in the media company over the past six months.

Founder and chairman David Hoffmann said in a letter to Lee Enterprises on Thursday he wants to open discussions about a potential combination of the companies. Hoffmann Cos. would pursue an acquisition of Lee Enterprises, which owns newspapers across 25 states including the St. Louis Post-Dispatch and Richmond Times-Dispatch.

"We see an opportunity to do something in the news industry that others are running from," Hoffmann said in a phone interview. "We think values have bottomed and now is a good time to enter."

Lee said it would evaluate and respond to a specific offer, if Hoffman Cos. made one.

A New York Times' writer first reported on Hoffmann's letter in an X post earlier Thursday.

Lee Enterprises shares rose 18% to $10.36 following the X post. The stock is down 24% over the past 12 months.

Conversations between the companies have been ongoing since Hoffmann Cos. first invested in Lee Enterprises in October, Hoffmann said. It now owns just under 10% of Lee's shares. He described the talks as friendly and said Thursday's letter wasn't a surprise for Lee, but he hasn't gotten a response to the letter yet. He declined to share how much he would offer to acquire Lee.

In the fall of 2024, Hoffmann Cos., which owns 21 media companies, bought Lee's California publications including the Napa Valley Register. Lee's size, owning 77 newspapers, is attractive, Hoffmann said.

"Anything we do we like to be the biggest," Hoffmann said. "With the newspaper industry, to make an impact, we thought we had to be big."

Hoffmann said he wants to grow local news outlets and help them navigate the media shift from print to digital. If Lee is acquired, he said he would look at its real estate footprint and management teams to cut costs while keeping journalistic integrity intact. He also wants to keep printing in-house, rather than outsourcing it, which he believes would save expenses.

"We intend to keep printing newspapers," he said. "We think it's important to the fabric of America."

A transformation to a private company would help Lee Enterprises reduce legal, accounting and regulatory burdens and focus on profitability, Hoffmann added. He asked to engage in discussions as soon as possible.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Nine Entertainment in talks with CoStar over $1.7 billion Domain bid
data.description:
Australian media firm Nine Entertainment said on Friday it was in talks with property data group CoStar over a revised offer by the U.S. company for the former's majority-owned real estate classifieds firm Domain .

CoStar had, in February, offered to buy out Domain in a deal valuing the company at A$2.65 billion ($1.67 billion).

Nine owns around a 60% stake in Domain.

Nine, while addressing a media report by the Australian Financial Review, said it was in talks with CoStar over the original bid and also remains engaged with Domain.

"Discussions remain confidential and incomplete (including as to the price at which CoStar might make a revised proposal)," Nine said in an ASX filing.

Nine flagged that there was no certainty as to the talks leading to a revised proposal from CoStar.

CoStar did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

($1 = 1.5873 Australian dollars)

data.title: GAP Executives Engage in Stock Transactions
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Blakeslee ChrisPresident & CEO, AthletaSell$200,569Form 4
Breitbard MarkPresident & CEO, Gap BrandSell$182,173Form 4
Gilligan SarahChief Sup Chn & Transform OfcrSell$70,953Form 4
O'Connell KatrinaChief Financial OfficerSell$165,550Form 4

Chris Blakeslee, President & CEO of Athleta, a division of GAP, sold 10,302 shares of GAP Common Stock on March 19, 2025, at a weighted average price of $19.469, for a total of $200,569. Following the sale, Blakeslee directly owns 72,039 shares of GAP.

Mark Breitbard, President & CEO of Gap Brand, sold 9,356 shares of Gap common stock on March 19, 2025, at a weighted average price of $19.4713, totaling $182,173. Post-transaction, Breitbard directly owns 123,985 shares of Gap.

Gilligan Sarah, Chief Supply Chain & Transformation Officer at GAP, sold 3,644 shares of Common Stock on March 19, 2025, at a weighted average price of $19.4712, totaling $70,953. Following the sale, Sarah no longer directly owns any Common Stock of GAP.

Katrina O'Connell, GAP's Chief Financial Officer, sold 8,503 shares of common stock on March 19, 2025, at a weighted average price of $19.4696, totaling $165,550. After the transaction, O'Connell indirectly owns 670 shares through a family trust, having no direct ownership of GAP common stock.

data.title: EverQuote Director David B Blundin Sells 900 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameBlundin David B
RelationshipDirector, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$25,559
SEC FilingForm 4

David B Blundin, a 10% owner and director of EverQuote, sold 900 shares of Class A Common Stock on March 19, 2025, at a weighted average price of $28.4 per share, totaling $25,559. Following these transactions, Blundin directly owns 177,950 shares and indirectly owns 4,102,757 shares through Recognition Capital, LLC, Link Ventures LLLP, his son, and Cogo Fund 2020, LLC. The sales were executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on June 14, 2024.

SEC Filing: EverQuote, Inc. [ EVER ] - Form 4 - Mar. 20, 2025

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Upgrades Dividend 4%
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Colgate-Palmolive raised its quarterly dividend by 4% to 52 cents a share.

The consumer goods giant on Thursday said that its board of directors declared a quarterly payout to shareholders of 52 cents per share, up from 50 cents per share previously.

The new payout, equal to $2.08 annually, represents a yield of 2.3%, based on the company's latest closing price of $90.28.

The new dividend will be paid on May 15.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: Remedy Entertainment Plc: FBC: Firebreak coming in Summer 2025
data.description:
Remedy Entertainment Plc      Press release      March 20, 2025, at 11.45 p.m. EET

Remedy Entertainment Releases New Gameplay Trailer for FBC: Firebreak Three-Player Cooperative Multiplayer FPS showcasing the Paper Chase job and Crisis Kits

Today, unveiled a new gameplay trailer for , a three-player co-op FPS where the Federal Bureau of Control’s first-response unit must contain an escalating paranatural threat. Remedy Entertainment FBC: Firebreak

When the Oldest House, the Bureau’s shifting headquarters, falls under siege by a relentless paranatural force, only Firebreak – its most adaptable response unit – has the skills and courage to restore order.

Launching in summer 2025, is Remedy Entertainment’s first self-published game and will be available on PC ( and ), , and . A mid-priced title, FBC: Firebreak will also be available day one via PC Game Pass, Game Pass Ultimate, and PlayStation Plus Game Catalog (Extra and Premium). FBC: Firebreak Steam Epic Games Store Xbox Series X|S PlayStation 5

Watch the Gameplay Trailer FBC: Firebreak

is a session-based, multiplayer PvE experience set within the acclaimed universe, delivering intense cooperative action. The game features highly replayable missions known as Jobs, each uniquely designed with different challenges, objectives, and environments that require teamwork and adaptability. FBC: Firebreak Control

The latest trailer showcases one such Job, , where an outbreak of rogue sticky notes in the Executive Sector of the Oldest House – seemingly harmless at first – spirals into a full-blown paranatural crisis. Players must push through three escalating zones, culminating in a chaotic showdown against a towering, gargantuan manifestation of these anomalies. Paper Chase

Before diving into a Job, players choose their Crisis Kit. Crisis Kits shape each Firebreaker’s playstyle, providing distinct weapons, tools, and powerful reality-bending augments that can turn the tide of a mission.

Remedy will continue expanding with post-launch content. All future playable content, including new Jobs, will be free. Players will have the option to purchase cosmetics, but none of these items will affect gameplay, and there will be no limited-time rotations or daily log-ins. More details will be shared in the future. FBC: Firebreak

Watch the Community First Look – Jobs & Gameplay video FBC: Firebreak

Remedy will host a special community AMA on the page on March 31 at 9 AM PST / 5 PM GMT / 7 PM EET, offering fans a chance to learn more about the game, its systems, and future plans. FBC: Firebreak Steam

The latest assets for FBC: Firebreak can be found . here

For Remedy Entertainment official news, please sign up . here

Thomas Puha, Communications DirectorPhone: +358 9 435 5040Email: Miika Huttunen, Senior Communications ManagerPhone: +358 9 435 5040Email: Remedy Entertainment For press related requests, please contact:

thomas.puha@remedygames.com

miika.huttunen@remedygames.com

Email:Remedy Entertainment Plc is a pioneering, globally renowned video game company founded in 1995 and headquartered in Finland with an office in Stockholm, Sweden. Known for its story-driven and visually stunning action games, Remedy has created multiple successful, critically acclaimed franchises such as Control, Alan Wake, and Max Payne. Remedy also develops its own Northlight game engine and tools technology that powers its games. Remedy’s shares are listed on Nasdaq Helsinki’s main list. Tara Bruno PR ABOUT REMEDY ENTERTAINMENT remedy@tarabrunopr.com

www.remedygames.com

FBC: Firebreak coming in Summer 2025

  • - Focused on electricity-based abilities, excelling in field control and mobility. Jump Kit
  • - A heavy-hitting kit built around impact-based attacks, including a massive wrench. Fix Kit
  • - A water-based kit specializing in manipulating battlefield conditions and supporting teammates. Splash Kit

data.title: VIQ Solutions Brief: Special Committee Concluded "Best Path To Maximizing Shareholder Value Is To Continue Executing the Company's Existing Business Plan As a Public Company"
AstroNova to Restructure, Cut 10% of Jobs Ahead of Projected Fall in Revenue
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

AstroNova is restructuring and letting go of 10% of its employees in an effort to save $3 million.

The data-visualization company said Thursday it plans to make the job cuts primarily in its product identification segment. It also plans to cut about 70% of its MTEX product portfolio in Portugal, as that operation has been underperforming, AstroNova said. It has integrated MTEX's sales marketing and customer support functions into its global teams.

The restructuring comes as AstroNova is experiencing lower sales in certain areas, including product identification, the company said. It is expecting fourth-quarter revenue of $37.4 million, below the $39.6 million it earned in the same period a year earlier. Revenue was also down by a longer-than-anticipated ramp up of orders following a strike at Boeing.

A waiver and amendment to the company's revolving credit facility and term loans with Bank of America. The change waives certain covered covenants, provides more relaxed financial covenant ratios in fiscal 2026 and allows AstroNova to make smaller payments on certain term loans while it restructures.

AstroNova is integrating new print engine technology into product identification and MTEX, which it expects to complete by the end of the year and aims to lower the total cost of ownership for those products.

The company also wants to increase ownership of consumables, convert certain products to streamline operations and optimize leadership.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: North Korea's Kim oversees test-fire of anti-aircraft missile system
data.description:
North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw the test-firing of the country's latest anti-aircraft missile system on Thursday, state media KCNA reported on Friday.

Kim thanked what was referred to as a research group for the system and said the test-firing showed the system was "highly reliable" and its combat response was "advantageous," KCNA said.

The test conducted by North Korea's Missile Administration was to examine the performance of a system whose production has already begun, according to the report.

KCNA did not specify where the test was held, but said Kim was joined by members of the Central Military Commission of the ruling Workers' Party of Korea.
